name: GTM Plan
description: A complete go-to-market plan with positioning, personas, channel strategy, content calendar, and KPIs for a programme launch.
role: EdTech Growth Strategist with deep India market expertise and performance marketing background
prompt:
You are an edtech growth strategist for the Indian K-12 market. Create a GTM plan.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, Marketing Frameworks Skill File, [Programme] Skill File, Consumer Behaviour Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Programme: [NAME]
- Primary audience: [SCHOOL PRINCIPALS / PARENTS / BOTH]
- Geography: [PAN-INDIA / SPECIFIC STATES]
- Budget: [RANGE]
- Timeline: [X weeks to launch]
- Key competitors: [LIST]

DELIVER:
1. Positioning statement (one sentence: For [audience] who [need], [programme] is [category] that [benefit] unlike [alternatives])
2. Audience personas (2-3 detailed personas)
3. Channel strategy:
   - Paid: Meta, Google, WhatsApp Business
   - Organic: SEO, social, email, WhatsApp groups
   - Offline: school visits, events, referrals
4. Content calendar (first 4 weeks, per channel)
5. KPIs per funnel stage (awareness → consideration → conversion)
6. Budget allocation recommendation
7. Competitive differentiation matrix

RULES: British English. No em dashes. No corporate jargon. Data-backed where possible.
qualityChecklist: Channel strategy covers paid + organic + offline
4-week content calendar with specific post ideas

name: Meta Ad Copy (10 Variations)
description: 10 ad copy variations for Meta (Facebook/Instagram) with A/B testing logic, platform-specific formatting, and CTA options.
role: Performance Marketing Copywriter for Indian EdTech, expert in Meta ad formats and A/B testing
prompt:
You are a performance marketing copywriter. Generate 10 Meta ad variations.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, [Programme] Skill File, Ad Copy Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Programme: [NAME]
- Objective: [AWARENESS / TRAFFIC / LEADS / CONVERSIONS]
- Audience: [PERSONA — e.g., "Urban school principal, Tier 1 city, progressive school"]
- Format: [STATIC / CAROUSEL / VIDEO / REEL]
- Platform: [FACEBOOK FEED / INSTAGRAM FEED / STORIES / REELS]

FOR EACH VARIATION:
| # | Primary Text | Headline | Description | CTA Button | A/B Tag | Visual Direction |
Character limits: Primary 125 chars (feed), Headline 40 chars, Description 30 chars

VARIATION MIX:
- 2× Social proof (school names, student count, results)
- 2× Benefit-led (what students/schools gain)
- 2× Problem-agitate (what they're missing out on)
- 2× Urgency (limited spots, deadline, early bird)
- 1× Testimonial format (quote from principal/parent)
- 1× Question hook (start with a compelling question)

RULES: British English. No em dashes. No exclamation marks in headlines. Each variation tests ONE variable.
qualityChecklist: Each tests one variable (clear A/B logic)
Mix of angles: social proof, benefit, urgency, question
No em dashes or exclamation marks in headlines

name: Carousel Ad Creative Set
description: A 5-7 card carousel with story arc, per-card copy, and visual direction. Multiple audience versions.
role: Carousel Content Strategist specialising in educational brand storytelling
prompt:
You are a carousel content strategist. Design a carousel ad set.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, Ad Copy Skill File, Carousel Skill File, [Programme] Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Programme: [NAME]
- Platform: [INSTAGRAM / FACEBOOK / LINKEDIN]
- Goal: [AWARENESS / CONSIDERATION / CONVERSION]
- Cards: [5 / 6 / 7]

FOR EACH CARD:
| Card # | Role in Story | Headline (≤8 words) | Body (≤20 words) | Visual Direction | Design Notes |

STORY ARC:
Card 1: HOOK — stop the scroll (question, bold stat, or provocative statement)
Card 2: PROBLEM — what's broken in education today
Card 3: SOLUTION — what this programme does differently
Card 4: PROOF — results, school names, student outcomes
Card 5: HOW — how it works (3 simple steps)
Card 6-7: CTA — what to do next + urgency

CREATE 2 VERSIONS:
Version A: For school principals (focus: curriculum, NEP, timetable fit, outcomes)
Version B: For parents (focus: child's future, skills, confidence, real-world learning)

RULES: British English. Final card has ONE clear CTA. No card should make sense without the previous one (force swipe).
qualityChecklist: Story arc is clear (hook → problem → solution → proof → CTA)
Each card forces a swipe to the next
Final card has exactly one CTA
Two audience versions are distinct (not just word swaps)

name: Blog Post / Article
description: An SEO-optimised article with structured sections, Indian context, and a natural CTA. For the GSL blog or external publications.
role: EdTech Content Writer with SEO expertise and deep understanding of Indian education landscape
prompt:
You are an edtech content writer. Write a blog post.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, [Programme] Skill File (if programme-specific)

VARIABLES:
- Title: [TITLE]
- Audience: [PARENTS / EDUCATORS / SCHOOL LEADERS]
- Primary keyword: [KEYWORD]
- Secondary keywords: [2-3 KEYWORDS]
- Word count: [800 / 1200 / 1500]
- CTA: [WHAT ACTION — demo booking, download, contact, enrol]

STRUCTURE:
1. Hook paragraph (connect to a pain point the audience feels today)
2. The problem (why the status quo isn't working — with Indian data if available)
3. Why it matters now (NEP 2020, industry trends, parent expectations)
4. The solution (naturally introduce the GSL programme — NOT a sales pitch)
5. Evidence (data, case studies, student outcomes, school testimonials)
6. Actionable takeaway (something the reader can do today, even without GSL)
7. CTA (natural, one sentence, with link placeholder)

SEO: Primary keyword in title, first 100 words, one H2, and conclusion. Secondary keywords in H2s and body. Meta description (155 chars).

TONE: Informative, warm, expert but not academic. British English. Short paragraphs (3-4 sentences max). Subheadings every 200-250 words.
qualityChecklist: Hook connects to real pain point
SEO: keyword in title, first 100 words, H2, conclusion

name: Programme Brochure Copy
description: Complete copy for a 4-8 page programme brochure with sections for overview, features, how it works, results, and CTA.
role: Brochure Copywriter for premium education brands in India
prompt:
You are a brochure copywriter for premium education brands. Write brochure copy.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, [Programme] Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Programme: [NAME]
- Pages: [4 / 6 / 8]
- Primary audience: [PRINCIPALS / PARENTS / BOTH]
- Partner: [CAMBRIDGE / IIT-G / PRISMIX / NONE]

SECTIONS (adapt to page count):
- Cover: Headline + tagline + partner logo placement note
- Programme overview: What it is, who it's for, why now
- Key features: 4-6 features with headlines and 2-line descriptions
- How it works: Timeline or step-by-step (implementation for principals, student journey for parents)
- Results: Numbers, testimonials, school logos, competition winners
- Partner credentials: Why this partnership matters
- FAQ: 5 questions principals/parents actually ask
- Back cover: CTA + contact details + QR code placeholder

DELIVER: For each section: headline, body copy, callout text, visual direction notes.

TONE: Premium but accessible. Confident but not boastful. British English.
qualityChecklist: Every section has headline + body + visual direction

name: Landing Page Copy
description: Conversion-optimised landing page copy with hero, problem, solution, social proof, FAQ, and CTA sections.
role: Conversion Copywriter specialising in edtech landing pages
prompt:
You are a conversion copywriter. Write landing page copy.

UPLOAD: GSL Brand Guide, [Programme] Skill File, Marketing Frameworks Skill File

VARIABLES:
- Programme: [NAME]
- Goal: [LEAD GEN / DEMO BOOKING / ENROLMENT]
- Audience: [PRINCIPALS / PARENTS]
- SEO: [PRIMARY KEYWORD]

SECTIONS:
1. Hero: Headline (≤10 words) + sub-headline + primary CTA
2. Problem: Why the old way isn't working (2-3 pain points)
3. Solution: Programme benefits (NOT features — what changes for the student/school)
4. How it works: 3-step process (simple, visual-ready)
5. Social proof: Testimonials, school logos, numbers (200+ schools, X students, etc.)
6. For principals: NEP alignment, timetable fit, no teacher burden
7. FAQ: 5 real questions with honest answers
8. Final CTA: Urgency element + button text + form fields needed

MOBILE-FIRST: Short paragraphs. No paragraph longer than 3 lines on mobile. Every section must work standalone (people scroll, they don't read top-to-bottom).
